checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document's structure closely for the sections provided. While it includes AI-generated headings and captions, these are explicitly permitted and based on the content of the document. No evidence of completely invented paragraphs or sentences was found.

I verified the phone numbers (e.g., (509) 324-4000), email addresses, URLs (e.g., http://sbgi.net/?p=1224), and mailing addresses (e.g., 500 W. Boone Ave Spokane, WA 99201) in the output against the source document. All contact information present in the output accurately reflects the source material.

The source document contains 46 pages. The conversion result explicitly states that 35 sections were omitted for token budget. Major portions of the document are missing, including pages 6-8, 10-33, and 35-42. This constitutes a massive loss of core information.
checks.b_evidence_source: 46 pages of content including FCC Fee Diversion Background, NG911 Program Areas, Glossary, and WA 911 Jobs marketing plans.

checks.a_analysis: The tables present in the converted sections (agenda, meeting attendees, vote counts, contract line items, and invoice spots) were checked for row counts. For instance, the agenda table correctly shows all 9 topics, and the KOMO contract table correctly shows all 4 advertisement lines (N1-N4).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While massive sections are missing (handled under C3), within the sections that were successfully converted, the content is largely intact. Splits in reading order (like the end of agenda item 3 being moved to a separate element) are acceptable reformatting and do not lose information.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are several text fidelity issues. First, in House Bill 203 (Page 34), the output loses the semantic meaning of the amendment by failing to mark '2021' as a strikethrough and '2022' as an underline, making the change in the law ambiguous (Major). Second, there are minor truncations in table data, such as 'MONDAY 10-1' instead of 'MONDAY 10-11P' (Page 43). Finally, extra characters like asterisks were added to the agenda items (Minor).
checks.b_evidence_location: House Bill 203 (element 446) and KOMO Contract Table (element 562)

checks.a_analysis: An examination of the output reveals that text blocks and images (such as the NC 911 Board logo and the KOMO logo) appear multiple times only when they are repeated in the source PDF (e.g., on multiple pages of the meeting minutes or separate contract documents). No unintentional duplication was found.

The heading hierarchy is well-maintained. The document starts with H1 for the board title, followed by H2 for the specific meeting title, and H3 for document components like 'DRAFT- Minutes'. Sub-sections like 'Meeting Attendees' are correctly identified as H4. Major sections in subsequent documents (House Bills, FCC documents) use H1 or H2 consistently.

Tables are correctly converted with structural integrity. Meeting attendee tables (Committee, Guests, Staff) preserve their respective column counts (4, 3, and 4). Complex tables in the contract and invoice sections (Pages 43-46) are broken into logical sub-tables (e.g., header info vs. line items), which preserves the relational structure of the data.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the source document. It correctly processes the agenda, the subsequent minutes, legislative attachments, and final invoices in the order they appear in the original file.

Lists, specifically those in the meeting minutes agenda and the 2021 goals sections, are converted accurately. Although some numbered lists are represented as unordered in the JSON metadata, the numeric markers are preserved in the text content, maintaining the list's meaning.
All URLs found in the source (FCC reports, government sites, station websites) are present in the text of the output. No broken, missing, or fabricated links were identified.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. Document sections, headings, and overall flow follow the original intent. Complex pages like the invoices and contracts are appropriately restructured into accessible HTML tables while maintaining their logical grouping. The infographic elements on page 37 are converted into a clear vertical layout which is an acceptable transformation for responsive HTML.

All tables, including the meeting attendee lists (page 2), voting results (pages 3-5), and complex financial forms (pages 43-46), are correctly rendered as HTML tables. Row and column structures are preserved, headers are distinct, and data remains perfectly readable and aligned.

While most images (logos, banner, analytics chart) are present, significant meaningful diagrams from page 38 of the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the 'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' flowchart and the 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' network diagram are absent, with only their text headings remaining. These are critical technical diagrams required for understanding the document content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 38 of the PDF contains two meaningful diagrams: 'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' (a circular flowchart) and 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' (a detailed network diagram).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, these sections contain only the text headings, and the corresponding diagrams are completely missing from the rendered content.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 38, 'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' and 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' sections.

checks.a_analysis: There are no instances of duplicated body content. While headings appear in both the navigation area/TOC and the body, this is an intentional accessibility feature. Paragraphs, tables, and lists are only rendered once in their appropriate locations within the document body.
